summary: Today's sweep leans toward AI / privacy, Democracy / press freedom, AI / computing. The strongest items in this run are Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI, Middle East crisis live: UAE says it has intercepted three Iran fired drones; US denies that Iran hit warship near strait of Hormuz, and Amazon’s trying to turn its massive shipping operation into another AWS.

mainItems.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
mainItems.summary: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: Middle East crisis live: UAE says it has intercepted three Iran fired drones; US denies that Iran hit warship near strait of Hormuz
mainItems.summary: Drones shot down by UAE would be first since ceasefire; US Central Command denies ship was struck after Iran claimed two missiles hit a US navy frigate Trump says US navy will ‘guide’ trapped ships from strait of Hormuz amid ‘very…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Amazon’s trying to turn its massive shipping operation into another AWS
mainItems.summary: Amazon might have a part in delivering all kinds of stuff to your door soon, as its massive shipping network opens up to other companies outside its marketplace, more directly competing with giants like DHL, UPS, and FedEx.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: AMD is adding HDMI 2.1 support for Linux. That's good news for the Steam Machine.
mainItems.summary: Fixed Rate Link being added now; Display Stream Compression coming soon.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Artius: Pure Imagination is Sonic the Hedgehog for art students in a post-AI world
mainItems.summary: A thing that kind of got lost somewhere along the way in the Sonic the Hedgehog series is its themes of environmentalism.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Week one of the Musk v. Altman trial: What it was like in the room
mainItems.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Musk’s “World War III” threat in Twitter lawsuit haunts him at OpenAI trial
mainItems.summary: OpenAI accuses Musk of trying to "coerce" a settlement days before trial started.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft Back Bill to Fund ‘AI Literacy’ in Schools
mainItems.summary: A new bill introduced by Senators Adam Schiff and Mike Rounds would award grants to the National Science Foundation—which has endured massive funding cuts under the Trump Administration for science research—to put “AI literacy” in schools.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: The rise of cosy gaming: is this the closest many young people will get to home ownership?
mainItems.summary: More than a quarter of 20- to 34-year-olds still live with their parents. No wonder they are escaping into virtual properties that they can decorate and furnish as they like Name: Cosy gaming.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Space Hauler is a full scale space flight sim set around Saturn where every single button in your ship is functional
mainItems.summary: I do love a bit of space exploration in games, there's something quite beautiful about the infinite openness, alongside having a terrifying primal fear quality.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Supreme Court gives abortion pill mifepristone a 1-week reprieve from a major change
mainItems.summary: The Supreme Court says rules that allow patients to get the abortion pill mifepristone through the mail can stay the same for at least a week.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: Kentucky’s Childcare Benefit for Early Educators Is Spreading Fast
mainItems.summary: Many early childhood educators can’t afford childcare for their own children — an irony that has long marked the early care and education field.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.summary: Lochley attempts to negotiate with the telepaths, and G'Kar finds an old friend in the Centauri palace... The post Babylon 5 Rewatch: “A Tragedy of Telepaths” appeared first on Reactor .

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

new.title: Constellation
new.why: A little more cerebral and mood-heavy, but it scratches the identity / reality-slippage itch.

new.title: The Devil’s Hour
new.why: Dark, twisty, and structurally ambitious without feeling like homework.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

older.title: Continuum
older.why: A cleaner old-school sci-fi fit if you want procedural momentum wrapped around time-travel politics.

older.title: Person of Interest
older.why: Starts procedural and then quietly becomes one of the smartest AI / surveillance shows on television.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: Solve a murder mystery by flying people around on your giant crane in the Disco Elysium and Chinese folktale inspired The Crane Rider's Tale
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing at James's request.

highlights.title: 12 unexpected sourdough bread recipes that aren't crusty loaves: From sourdough flatbread to sourdough milk bread — and even sourdough babka.
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing, the kind of bread-nerd rabbit hole worth keeping.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
